title: Alon et al. Conjecture on Separation Dimension and Maximum Degree
statement_text: Every graph with maximum degree $\Delta$ has separation dimension $O(\Delta)$.
context_text: Chandran et al. proved an upper bound of $2\Delta(\lceil\log_2\log_2\Delta\rceil+3)+1$, and Alon et al. improved this to $29\log^*(\Delta)\cdot\Delta$. Alon et al. conjectured that a linear bound $O(\Delta)$ should hold. The present paper proves this conjecture, showing the bound $20\Delta$ is best possible up to a constant factor.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture by establishing that every graph with maximum degree $\Delta > 1$ has separation dimension less than $20\Delta$ (Theorem 1), which matches the known lower bound of $\Delta/2$ for almost every $\Delta$-regular graph up to a constant factor.
notes: Original conjecture statement reconstructed from prose description; no verbatim LaTeX available for the conjecture itself. PDF source — math may be garbled.

title: Alon et al. Open Problem on Bounded Separation Dimension and Degeneracy
statement_text: Do graphs with bounded separation dimension have bounded degeneracy (equivalently, bounded average degree)?
context_text: Every graph with separation dimension at most 2 is planar and has average degree less than 6. For general $s > 3$, Alon et al. proved that $n$-vertex graphs with separation dimension $s$ have average degree $O(\log^{s-2} n)$, and asked whether bounded separation dimension forces bounded degeneracy. The present paper addresses the first open case $s = 3$.
paper_contribution: Partially resolves the problem by proving (Theorem 2) that there exists a constant $c$ such that every graph with separation dimension 3 has average degree at most $c$, and additionally that such graphs have bounded chromatic number.
notes: Problem described in prose without a labelled theorem environment. PDF source — full Section 4 proof text is truncated; additional conjectures or questions in the remainder of the paper may not be captured.
